title: Person killed in Mattapan shooting, 2 others shot in Dorchester overnight Saturday
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was killed in a shooting outside 28 Woolson St. in Mattapan on Saturday night.
Two people were shot near Geneva Avenue and Everton Street in Dorchester early Sunday morning.
No arrests have been made in the Dorchester shooting; investigations into both incidents are ongoing.

title: Kevin Garnett wasn’t a fan of Jayson Tatum missing Game 7 vs. Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Garnett expressed surprise at Jayson Tatum being ruled out for Game 7 against the Sixers due to knee stiffness.
Despite appearing fit in Game 6, Tatum was unexpectedly deemed questionable before being ruled out hours ahead of Game 7.
Tatum cited knee tightness after returning from a long absence, with Boston prioritizing a long-term decision over immediate play.

title: 2 people wounded, 1 seriously, after shooting in Dorchester
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Two people were shot on Kingsdale Street in Dorchester; a woman has life-threatening injuries, and a man has non-life-threatening injuries.
Boston police received multiple 911 calls about the shooting at 69 Kingsdale St. around 6:40 p.m. on Sunday night.
Police are investigating the shooting, canvassing for witnesses, and there have been no arrests; the public is not believed to be in danger.

title: NBA insider: Atlanta Hawks were interested in trade for Jaylen Brown last year
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was a top MVP candidate and is under contract for three more years with the Boston Celtics.
The Atlanta Hawks discussed a potential trade for Brown last summer, but concerns over his contract halted progress.
Brown expressed continued commitment to Boston despite swirling trade rumors and reported frustration with the team.

title: Woman charged in Southborough fatal crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Abigail Throckmorton, 23, charged with motor vehicle homicide in Southborough crash killing Crystal Johnson-Clementi.
Throckmorton faces charges of negligent operation, speeding, and driving the wrong way; pleaded not guilty.
Throckmorton released on personal recognizance with conditions; pretrial hearing set for June 30.

title: What’s causing an engine shake in my 10-year-old SUV?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2016 Audi Q5 with highway engine shake and warning lights likely has a misfiring engine.
A flashing check engine light on an Audi suggests immediate towing to prevent further damage.
Car fault lights include EPC indicating a throttle system or electronic issue with potential engine misfire.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox fall flat against Rays on Mother’s Day
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle struggled, allowing three runs on seven hits in a 4-1 loss to the Rays.
Trevor Story went 1-for-4 with a double but continued his slump, striking out three times to drop his average to .200.
Catcher Mickey Gasper shone, going 3-for-4 with an RBI, providing a rare bright spot in Boston's otherwise lackluster offense.

title: Boston gang members, associates facing slew of firearm, drug trafficking charges
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Eight defendants linked to Boston gangs face firearm and drug trafficking charges in a federal indictment.
Charges include conspiracy to deal firearms without a license and distribution of fentanyl and crack cocaine.
A federal investigation recovered 18 firearms, conversion devices, and large amounts of fentanyl and crack cocaine.

title: Photos: Scenes from a fun, but soggy, Saturday in Greater Boston
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: LEGO Group's Duckling Day event took place on Boston Common, featuring duck-themed activities with LEGO® DUPLO® bricks.
Somerville hosted PorchFest with free music performed on porches across the city, attracting large crowds despite the rain.
Mojo Boston Music Festival showcased a 10-hour event of contemporary music and urban art at City Hall Plaza.

title: New Bedford uncle pleads guilty to killing nephew during family altercation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Juan Padilla-Santana, 43, pleaded guilty to manslaughter for the death of his nephew Omil Padilla-Corsino in New Bedford.
Padilla-Santana was sentenced to 12½ to 17½ years in prison after the fatal stabbing during a family altercation.
Prosecutors said Padilla-Santana made misleading statements; no evidence indicated the nephew was armed during the incident.
